title: Types of business entity
summary: 9706 P2 — sole trader, partnership, limited company: liability, finance, and reporting.
learningObjectives: Compare sole trader, partnership, and limited company structures.
Explain advantages and disadvantages of each entity type.
Outline ownership, liability, and finance differences.
Relate entity type to financial reporting requirements.

simpleExplanation.title: Who owns the business?
simpleExplanation.summary: Entity type determines legal liability, how finance is raised, and which financial statements must be prepared and published.
steps: Sole trader — one owner, unlimited liability, simplest accounts.
Partnership — shared ownership; appropriation account needed.
Limited company — separate legal personality, limited liability.
Companies face stricter disclosure and audit requirements.

flashcards.back: Trading account, SPL, SOFP — no published accounts required.
Appropriation account after net profit on SPL.
Company owns assets; shareholders not liable beyond investment.
Amount received above nominal value on share issue (1.5.4).
Drawings reduce sole trader/partner capital; dividend reduces company retained earnings.
Same ownership forms — 9706 focuses on accounting treatment.

sections.content: **Types of business entity** (1.1.1) opens **9706 Paper 2**. Examiners link **structure → accounts prepared**.

sections.question: Two accountants start a practice together, sharing profits 60:40. Which entity type and which extra financial statement is required compared to a sole trader?
sections.solution:
**Entity:** **Partnership** — deed should state **60:40 profit ratio**.

**Extra statement:** **Appropriation account** after SPL — deduct **salaries/interest on capital** if any, then **share residual profit** 60:40.

**SOFP:** Show **capital accounts** and **current accounts** per partner (1.5.3).

**Not a company** unless they incorporate — unlimited liability unless LLP structure (syllabus: general partnership).
